chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Criminal Bail Application No. 688 of 2024 (Abdul Rehman v. The State) Date Order with signature of Judge 1. For orders on MA No.3899/2024 2. For hearing of bail application 23.05.2024 Mr. Abbas Ali Shar, advocates for the applicant Mr. Muhammad Anwar Mahar, DDPP for the State ----------------------------------- It is alleged that the applicant with the rest of the culprits robbed complainant Mohsin Ali and P.W Musawar of their cell phones and Rs.20,000/-, for which the present case was registered. The applicant having been refused bail by learned IVth-Additional Sessions Judge, Karachi, Malir, has sought the same from this Court by way of instant bail application under Section 497 Cr.P.C. Heard arguments and perused the record. The FIR of the incident has been lodged with a delay of about one day; it does not contain the name of the applicant. The applicant has been subjected to an identification parade with a delay of about nine days to his actual arrest. No explanation for such delay is offered. The recovery is alleged by the applicant to have been foisted upon him by the police by arranging the same from the local market. The case has finally been challaned and there is no likelihood of absconsion or tampering with the evidence on the part of the applicant who is said to be in custody for six months without effective progress in the trial of his case. In these circumstances, a case for the release of the applicant on bail on point of further inquiry is made out. Under the given circumstances, the applicant is admitted to bail subject to his furnishing surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- (Rupees Fifty Thousand only) and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the learned trial Court.

In these circumstances, a case for the release of the applicant on bail on point of further inquiry is made out. Under the given circumstances, the applicant is admitted to bail subject to his furnishing surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- (Rupees Fifty Thousand only) and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the learned trial Court. Instant bail application is disposed of accordingly. J U D G E
